by
  classical
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · intro z
    refine TensorProduct.induction_on z ?h0 ?hadd ?htmul
    · simpa using (isCentral_zero : IsCentral (A ⊗[k] B) 0)
    · intro x y hx hy
      simpa [map_add] using hx.add hy
    · intro a b
      refine IsCentral.mul ?_ ?_
      · -- `a ⊗ₜ 1` is central
        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
        · intro y
          refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?y0 ?yadd ?ytmul
          · simp
          · intro y₁ y₂ hy₁ hy₂
            simpa [mul_add, add_mul] using congrArg2 (HAdd.hAdd) hy₁ hy₂
          · intro a' b'
            simp [TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
        · intro y
          refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?y0 ?yadd ?ytmul
          · simp
          · intro y₁ y₂ hy₁ hy₂
            simpa [mul_add, add_mul] using congrArg2 (HAdd.hAdd) hy₁ hy₂
          · intro a' b'
            simp [TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
      · -- `1 ⊗ₜ b` is central since `b` is central over `k`
        rcases (Algebra.isCentral_iff.mp (show IsCentral B b from (Algebra.IsCentral.isCentral b))) with
          ⟨hbL, hbR⟩
        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
        · intro y
          refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?y0 ?yadd ?ytmul
          · simp
          · intro y₁ y₂ hy₁ hy₂
            simpa [mul_add, add_mul] using congrArg2 (HAdd.hAdd) hy₁ hy₂
          · intro a' b'
            -- use centrality of `b` to commute in the `B`-factor
            have : b * b' = b' * b := by simpa [hbL b'] using hbL b'
            simp [TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, this, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
        · intro y
          refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?y0 ?yadd ?ytmul
          · simp
          · intro y₁ y₂ hy₁ hy₂
            simpa [mul_add, add_mul] using congrArg2 (HAdd.hAdd) hy₁ hy₂
          · intro a' b'
            have : b' * b = b * b' := by simpa [hbR b'] using hbR b'
            simp [TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, this, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  · intro a
    ext b
    simp

classical
  refine ⟨?_⟩
  intro a

classical
  -- `A` is central in `A ⊗[k] B` because `A` acts via the left tensor factor and hence commutes
  -- with all elements coming from the right tensor factor; the remaining commutation is inside `A`.
  refine ⟨?_⟩
  intro a x
  refine TensorProduct.induction_on x ?h0 ?htm ?hadd
  · simpa using (mul_zero (algebraMap A (A ⊗[k] B) a))
  · intro a' b'
    -- reduce to pure tensors and use commutation of left/right tensor factors
    -- plus commutativity inside `A`
    -- `algebraMap A (A ⊗[k] B) a = TensorProduct.includeLeft (R:=k) (A:=A) (B:=B) a`
    -- so we can compute on tensors.
    simp [Algebra.smul_mul_assoc, Algebra.mul_smul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  · intro x y hx hy
    simpa [mul_add, add_mul, hx, hy]
  ·
    -- helper for induction base case
    simpa
  ·
    -- helper for induction add step
    intro x y hx hy
    simpa [mul_add, add_mul, hx, hy]
  ·
    -- helper for induction tensor step
    intro a' b'
    simp [TensorProduct.mul_tmul, TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]

classical
  refine ⟨?_⟩

  intro y
